California
Fair Political
｀多′
Practices Commission
May ■0, ■990
L. G. Stathan
1008 10th street #514
Sacramento, CA 9 5814
Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. f-9 0-2 4 6
Dear l,Is . Statham :
You have requested advice 'on behalf of a nonprofit corpora-
tion to be formed. You wish to know if an el-ected officer can
engage in certain activities with respect to the corporation
consistent with the provisions of the Politica1 Reform Act (the
rrag1rr1.l Because your questions are general in nature and do not
involve a specific decision to be made under the Act, ^we are
treating yoirr regr""t as one for infornal assistance.2
OUESTIONS
1. can an elected officer solicit 
Inoney on behaLf of the
corporat ion?
2. can an elected officer donate his services as an an-
nouncer on behalf of the corporation?
CONCLUSIONS
1. The elected officer can solicit 
donations on behalf of
the nonprofit corporation. The corporation, as described, is
presuned not to be a controlled comrnittee.
・  GOVernment COde Sections 8■000-9■0■5.  Al■ statutory references
are to the Covernment code unlesS Otherw■
se ■ndicatedo  Commiss■on
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
■8000′  et seq.  Al■ references to regu■ations are to Tit■e 2′
Division 6 of the California Code of Regu■
ations.
2  .nforma■ assiStance does not rrovide the requestor w■th the
immunity provided by an opinion or forma■
 written advice.
(Government Code Section 83■■4′  2 Ca■. Code of Regs.
section ■8329(c)(3)。
)
428 J Strcet,Suite 800 0 P.0.Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804´0807 ● (916)322‐ 5660


Our F■■e No. ■-90-246
Page 2
2. The elected officer can donate his services as an an-
nouncer on behalf of the nonprofit corporation. Ilowever,
advertisements which feature the elected officer may be contribu-
tions to the etected officer by the nonprofit corporatj.on.
FACTS
A nonprofit corporation is beinq formed as a tax-exempt
organization under Section 501(c) (3) of the Internal Revenue code.
The corporation is formed for educational purposes. It will not
be run -on a day to day basis by a candidate or elected officer.
Its name will not incorporate the name of a candidate or eLected
officer. 
It rrill not engage in lobbying. It will not make
contributions to candidates or to con'nittees which support initia-
tives. 
The corporation witl have three directors. 
one director
will be the spouse of a candidate. The spouse wiII also be an
empl-oyee of the corPoration.
ANALYSIS
The involvement of an elected officer in the affairs of a
nonprofit organization can result in the organization being
trelted as a controlled committee of the elected officer in
certain circunstances. Because efected offi-cers often are
j.nvolved with nonprofit organizations, the Conmission recently
adopted a regulation that sets forth the circumstances in which a
nonlrofit organization is considergd a controlled comnittee.
lnelutation iezfi, copy enclosed. 1 3
The regrulation provides, in part, as follows:
(a) A nonprofit organization, as defined in
subdivision (f), shal1 be considered a controlled
conmittee, if both of the following apply:
(1) A candidate,l his or her agent, or
any conmittee he or she controls, exercises
significant influence over the actions and
deEisions of the organization, or acts jointly
$rith the organj-zation in connection with the
rnaking of expenditures.
3 Regulati on LB2L7 was adopted by the Conmission at its I'larch 13,
1990 freeting. The regulation is currently in the ruJ'emaking
proa"==. H6wever, it represents the policy of the cornmission, and
i" .r" issuing advice on the basis of the language of the regula-
tion effective APril 24, 1990.
4 An elected officer continues to be a candidate until his or her
status as a candidate is terminated pursuant to section a42]_4 '


Our File No. 
■-90-246
Page 3
. 
(2t The organization gualifies as a com_
nittee under covernuent Cod; Section 82013 (a) ,
and the organization is operated for pofiticii
purposes. _ For purposes of this regmlati,on, an
organization is rtoperated for poli{.icaI
purposestt if either of the following applies:
(A) The organization receives or
expends funds for the purpose of
influencing or attenpting to influence
the action of the voters for or against
the nonination or election of a cindidate
or the qualification or passage of any
measure.
(B) The organization nakes
contributions to candidates or their
controlled conmittees.
***
-- 
(f). For purposes of this regulation,
rrnonprof it organizationrr means an organization
which is characterized as an exempt 6rganization
under Section 501 of the Internal Revenue Code, but
which is not an organization fomed or existing
prinarily for political 
purposes.
The regmlation also provides guidance as to when a nonprofit
organization is presumed not to be a controlled conmittee
notwithstanding a candidate's invorvement. A nonprofit organiza-
tion which is tax-exenpt under section 501(c) (3) ;f the rnfernal
lgvenue code is pres,med not to be controried'by a candidate if
(1) the candidate j.s not substantially involved in the day_to_day
operations of the organization; (2) the organizati,on has i boaro
of directors with 3 or nore nembers, two-tf,irds of whom are not
candidates, agents, staff or enployees of candidates, or specified
relatives of a candidate who is a Loard member; ana irl tnl
candidate's name is not included in the nane oi the organization.
(Regmlation 18217 (b) . )
Even if a candidate exercises control over a nonprofit
orqanization, it will not be considered a controlled Lommittee
unless it is operated for poJ.itical purposes. We presune that
most. tax-exempt nonprofit organizations are not oplrated for
political 
purposes if they do not make contriEftions to
candidates, spend si.gnif icant amounts to support or oppose ba11ot
neasures or influence legisJ,ative action, oi- incluae Lfre
candidaters narue in the name of the organization. (Regulation
t82L7 (c).)
- 
According to the facts that you provided, we lirould presume
that the nonprofit corporation described in your Letter iould not
be a controlled comnittee. The corporation is to be tax-exempt


our File No. I-9 0-2 4 5
Page 4
under Section 501(c) (3) of the Internal Revenue Code. You state
that tero-thirds of the board of directors will be I'public'r
members. By ttpublictr we assume that you mean they will not be
agents, staff or relatives of candidates. A candidate,s naure is
not in the name of the corporation. The corporation will not make
contributions to candidates or spend funds to influence legisla-
tive action or to support or oppose bal1ot measures.
This presunption is not altered by the fact that the spouse
of the elected officer is employed by the organization. However,
if the spouse is acting as the aqent of the elected officer, then
the etected officer may, in fact, be substantially i.nvolved in the
day to day activities of the corporation, so as to negate the
presunption. we have previously advised you that the employnent
of the spouse nay affect the abitity of the elected officer to
nake donations or loans to the nonprofit corporation with campaign
funds. (Statharn Advice Letter No. I-89-690, coPy enclosed.)
The mere fact that an elected officer solicits donati.ons for
the organization does not nean that those donations are rrcontribu-
tions.ti 
A person who nakes a donation to a nonprofit organization
which is tax-exempt under Section 501(c) (3) is not naking a
political 
contribution unless the person knows or has reason to
know that the organization is operated for political 
purposes.
(Regnrlation 18217 (e) . )
An elected off j-cer rnay donate his services as an announcer on
behalf of the corporation. Holrever, under some circu:nstances this
could result in a contribution to the candidate by the nonprofit
corporat j.on. For example, if the elected officer donated his
services as an announcer for the corporation in an election year,
and the corporation paid for an advertisement which featured the
elected officer, the cost of the advertisement could be considered
a contrj-bution to the elected officer. 
(See, Mccarthv Advice Let-
ter No. A-90-163, coPY enclosed.)
I trust that this letter provides you with the infornation
that you need. If you have any further questions, please contact
me at (916) 322-59oL.
s incerely,
Kathryn E. Donovan
General counsel
?:ry/d//L
By: llargaret
counsel ,
w. Ell-ison
Legal Division
KED:MWE: aA
Enclosures
